meta.purpose: Scaffold for external validation references. Numbers should only be added when a trustworthy external source is attached.

statusLegend.captured: Reference source and expected outputs are attached and can be executed automatically.
statusLegend.partial: Some reference logic exists, but the case is not yet fully documented as an external benchmark.
statusLegend.scaffold: Case intentionally defined without benchmark numbers yet.

benchmarkClassLegend.external-captured: Captured benchmark tied to an external reference or published closed-form source.
benchmarkClassLegend.internal-captured: Captured benchmark tied to an internal derivation, executable harness, or repository-backed worksheet, not an external validation source.
benchmarkClassLegend.scaffold: Placeholder case shape exists but expected benchmark outputs are intentionally not locked yet.

cases.title: Single span simply supported under UDL
cases.goal: Validate baseline reactions, shear diagram, moment diagram for the solver.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-01 — Single span simply supported under UDL' (classical simply supported beam formulas: R_A = R_B = qL/2, M(L/4) = 3qL^2/32, M(L/2) = qL^2/8)
referenceSource.note: Repository note explicitly lists the closed-form reference formulas used for this benchmark. Inputs: L = 6000 mm, q = 5 N/mm.

tolerance.notes: 0.5% for force results; near-zero outputs use tight absolute tolerance.

cases.title: Single span simply supported with midspan point load
cases.goal: Validate point-load assembly and shear jump at the load position.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-02 — Single span simply supported with midspan point load' (classical formulas: reactions = P/2, M(mid) = PL/4, zero end moments)
referenceSource.note: Repository note explicitly lists the closed-form reference formulas used for this benchmark. Inputs: L = 6000 mm, P = 12000 N at midspan.

cases.title: Single span simply supported with eccentric point load
cases.goal: Validate unsymmetrical reactions and deflection convergence near the load point.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-03 — Single span simply supported with eccentric point load' (classical formulas: left/right reactions = Pb/L and Pa/L; deflection at load uses closed-form simply supported point-load expression)
referenceSource.note: Using L = 6000 mm, P = 12000 N, a = 2500 mm from left support, b = 3500 mm from right support. Deflection benchmark is executed with refined mesh (16 elements) per matrix guidance.

tolerance.notes: 0.5% for reactions/moment; 2% for deflection with refined mesh.

cases.title: UDL deflection convergence benchmark
cases.goal: Separate exact statics from displacement convergence behavior while capturing a truthful refined-mesh acceptance target.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-04 — UDL deflection convergence benchmark' together with classical Euler-Bernoulli simply supported beam formulas (w(L/2) = 5qL^4 / 384EI, w(L/4) = 57qL^4 / 6144EI)
referenceSource.note: This is an internal convergence benchmark, not a claim that coarse-mesh displacement is exact. Expected values are closed-form Euler-Bernoulli deflections evaluated using a refined executable mesh policy already demonstrated in regression: 2 elements are sufficient for midspan and 4 elements are sufficient for quarter-span under this solver formulation. The executable captured case therefore uses 4 elements to satisfy both outputs honestly.

tolerance.notes: 2% on deflection against closed-form Euler-Bernoulli values, with meshElements=4 chosen as the documented refined-mesh convergence target for simultaneously checking L/4 and L/2. Monotonic convergence behavior remains verified in the dedicated regression benchmark test.

cases.title: Two-span equal continuous beam under equal UDL
cases.goal: Validate interior support moment, positive span moment, and symmetric reactions for continuous behavior.

referenceSource.citation: Structural Basics, '2 Span Continuous Beam – Moment and shear force formulas due to different loads', case 'Uniformly distributed line load (UDL) – 2 Span continuous beam – formulas' (M_support = -qL^2/8, M_positive = 9qL^2/128 at x = 3L/8 from each outer support, R_outer = 3qL/8, R_internal = 5qL/4)
referenceSource.note: Using per-span length L = 4000 mm and q = 3 N/mm on both equal spans. Solver sign convention stores opposite end-action signs on adjacent elements, so expected benchmark uses physical support moment as negative hogging and symmetric positive span moment magnitude.

tolerance.notes: 0.5% on moment/reaction outputs; moment-location checks use ±1 mm absolute because the closed-form location is exact for this benchmark.

cases.title: Two-span equal continuous beam under UDL on one span only
cases.goal: Validate asymmetric continuous-beam redistribution and uplift-sign behavior when only one span carries UDL.

referenceSource.citation: Structural Basics, '2 Span Continuous Beam – Moment and shear force formulas due to different loads', case 'Uniformly distributed line load (UDL) on one span – 2 Span continuous beam – formulas' (M_support = -qL^2/16, M_positive_loaded = 49qL^2/512, R_left = 7qL/16, R_internal = 5qL/8, R_right = -qL/16)
referenceSource.note: Executable capture is now enabled because the solver/executor path accepts span-specific distributed load input while preserving the legacy scalar qDown fallback. Locked fixture uses L = 4000 mm per span, q = 3 N/mm on the left span only, and zero UDL on the right span. The executable check locks the repository solver's sign/location convention: loaded-span sagging moment is benchmarked against the published coefficient, while the unloaded span is verified to remain non-sagging (max positive moment = 0) under this implementation convention.

tolerance.notes: 0.5% on moment/reaction outputs against the published equal-two-span one-sided-UDL coefficients.

cases.title: Two-span equal continuous beam with one midspan point load on the left span
cases.goal: Validate one-sided point-load transfer, hogging support moment, and far-support uplift sign in a continuous beam.

referenceSource.citation: Structural Basics, '2 Span Continuous Beam – Moment and shear force formulas due to different loads', case 'Point load at midspan – 2 Span continuous beam – formulas' (M_support = -3QL/32, M_positive_loaded = 13QL/64, R_loaded_outer = 13Q/32, R_internal = 11Q/16, R_far_outer = -3Q/32)
referenceSource.note: Using per-span length L = 4000 mm and Q = 12000 N at the left-span midspan (x = 2000 mm). The source page is drawn with the point load on the opposite span relative to the repository fixture, so this executable capture mirrors the magnitudes into left-to-right solver coordinates; the far outer support therefore carries the negative reaction in this fixture ordering.

tolerance.notes: 0.5% on moment/reaction outputs; the loaded-span maximum occurs exactly at the load point for this benchmark, so location uses ±1 mm absolute tolerance.

cases.title: Thermal-only equal two-span internal capture
cases.goal: Validate thermal fixed-end action assembly, solver sign convention, and equal-two-span symmetry for the repository thermal load model without claiming external validation.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-08 — Thermal-only equal two-span internal capture'; notes/thermal-two-span-derivation.md, sections 'Relation to VM-06' and 'Bottom line'; notes/thermal-two-span-independent-harness.md, sections 'Conclusion for VM-06 readiness' and 'Recommended next step'; tests/helpers/thermalTwoSpanIndependentHarness.mjs
referenceSource.note: This is an internal captured benchmark for the locked equal-two-span thermal-only fixture. Expected values are backed by the repository derivation plus an independent reduced-system executable harness, and are intentionally not presented as an external validation benchmark. Locked fixture: spanMm=4000 per span, EI=1.5e13 N·mm², GA=1e18 N, kappaShear=1, thermalMoment=1000 N·mm on both spans, qDown=0, no point loads, all vertical supports restrained.

tolerance.notes: Near-exact tolerance because this is a deterministic internal derivation-backed harness capture, not a mesh-convergence benchmark. Zero outer support moment uses absolute tolerance.

implementationHints.captureNotes: Benchmark class is internal-captured on purpose: derivation-backed / implementation-backed internal benchmark for the locked fixture, not external validation.

cases.title: Sandwich section property sanity case
cases.goal: Validate section properties independently from the global solver.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-07 — Sandwich section property sanity case'; src/calc/section.js (`buildSectionProperties`, `calcSelfWeight_kPa`)
referenceSource.note: This is an internal sanity benchmark, not an external code-standard benchmark. Expected values are derived directly from the repository's section-property equations for a fixed geometry/material worksheet: panelWidth=1000 mm, coreThickness=80 mm, skinOut=0.5 mm, skinIn=0.4 mm, coreShearModulus=3.2 N/mm², compressiveModulus=4.5 N/mm², coreDensity=42 kg/m³, wrinklingMode=yield-only.

tolerance.notes: Tight tolerance because this is a deterministic repository derivation sanity case, not a mesh-sensitive benchmark.

cases.title: Capacity and governing-case bookkeeping case
cases.goal: Validate reporting layer and governing-case selection against a practical engineering worksheet.

referenceSource.citation: notes/external-validation-matrix.md, section 'VM-10 — Capacity / governing-case bookkeeping case'; src/calc/runPanelAnalysis.js; src/calc/checks.js; src/calc/reporting.js
referenceSource.note: This is an internal bookkeeping benchmark, not an external vendor or code-standard worksheet. Expected values are locked from a fully specified repository execution path with external panel, spans=[3,3] m, supportWidths=[20,20,20] mm, screwStrength=20 kN, deflectionLimit=L/1000, no point loads, and declared wrinkling stress 120 MPa so crushing clearly governs the final report ledger.

tolerance.notes: Tight ratio tolerance because this is a locked repository ledger case; governing label and pass/fail must match exactly.

cases.title: Redistribution trigger case
cases.goal: Validate simplified redistribution behavior without pretending exact closed-form equivalence.

referenceSource.note: Trend-based acceptance only until a stronger external reference exists.

tolerance.notes: Exact numeric benchmark intentionally not defined yet
